operating_state: Net sales increased 9.4% to $39.9 billion driven by SRS acquisition contributing $2.6 billion and favorable seasonal timing from fiscal calendar shift. Comparable sales declined 0.3% reflecting decreased customer transactions offset by flat average ticket, pressured by macroeconomic uncertainties and high interest rates. Online sales represented 15.5% of total sales, increasing 10.9% including seasonal benefit.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed to 33.8% from 34.1% primarily due to SRS inclusion, partially offset by lower shrink and supply chain productivity improvements. SG&A increased to 18.9% of sales from 18.3% due to absence of prior year legal benefit and higher payroll costs. Depreciation and amortization increased 15.9% driven by SRS intangible asset amortization.
material_events: Company opened three new U.S. stores bringing total count to 2,350. Quarterly dividend increased 2.2% to $2.30 per share with $2.3 billion paid to shareholders. Company repaid $1.1 billion of long-term debt and established new $7.0 billion backup credit facilities supporting commercial paper program.
macro_exposures: Foreign exchange headwinds negatively impacted net sales by $275 million due to stronger U.S. dollar. Company estimates over 50% of product purchases are domestically sourced with ongoing supply chain diversification efforts. Tariff and trade policy developments have not had meaningful financial impact but remain subject to ongoing policy discussions.
forward_outlook: Management expects approximately $4 billion in capital expenditures for fiscal 2025, representing 2.5% of anticipated net sales, focused on customer experience improvements and technology. Share repurchases remain paused through fiscal 2025 following SRS acquisition with $11.7 billion authorization remaining available.
uncertainty_profile: Macroeconomic uncertainties and persistently high interest rates continue pressuring home improvement demand with unpredictable impact on comparable sales performance. Trade policy discussions remain ongoing with uncertain ultimate impact despite supply chain diversification efforts. ROIC declined to 31.3% from 37.1% primarily due to SRS acquisition financing.
